commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e9ad8b8c4715a224a63ba9f5819e46853700d6ee)
commands.review_summary:
The PR migrates exec policy configuration from legacy `tools.exec.security` / `tools.exec.ask` keys to canonical `tools.exec.mode`, adding `always` and `full-always` modes across doctor, CLI, runtime, Codex, policy, UI, docs, and tests.

PR surface: Source +216, Tests +369, Docs -20. Total +565 across 68 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: migrate `tools.exec: { host: "sandbox", ask: "always" }`, then run sandbox exec with the migrated config; the runtime guard sees `defaults.mode === "deny"` and throws `exec denied`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] There is a narrow mechanical blocker in doctor migration and regression coverage that an automated repair can attempt, while maintainers still own the broader compatibility/security review.
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger
